runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "Python", "build_system": "pip", "package_manager": "pip", "build_commands": ["python -m build --sdist", "python -m build --wheel"], "install_commands": ["apt-get update", "apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential python3-dev", "rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "3.12", "system_packages": ["build-essential", "python3-dev"]}, "repo_type": "library", "base_image": "python:3.12.13-bookworm", "base_image_rationale": "Official Python base image with Python 3.12 on Debian Bookworm, suitable for compiling and packaging Biopython.", "pitfalls": ["CI uses Python 3.14 in matrix while base image is pinned to 3.12; ensure code is compatible across versions or adjust base image.", "Biopython builds include C extensions; ensure required build tools and headers are available; not all environments will have compatible prebuilt wheels"], "notes": "CI workflow builds a tarball and wheels from source, installs via pip, and runs tests including numpy; this blueprint targets a pure Python build with optional compiled extensions."}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# System dependencies for building Biopython (C extensions, etc.)
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential python3-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set workdir to build in source tree
WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository source code
COPY . /src

# Build both source tarball and wheel from the source
RUN python -m build --sdist
RUN python -m build --wheel

# Install the freshly built distribution(s)
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir dist/*

# Optional: clean up build artifacts to keep the final image small
RUN rm -rf dist Biopython.egg-info __pycache__ tests .tox \
    */__pycache__ */*.pyc

# Default command: quick import test to verify installation
CMD ["bash", "-lc", "python -c 'import Bio, sys; print(\"Biopython version:\", Bio.__version__)'"]
"}

{"path": ".dockerignore", "content": "__pycache__/
*.pyc
.dist/
build/
dist/
*.egg-info/
.eggs/
.eggs-info/
*.tox/
.cache/
*.pytest_cache/
.DS_Store
node_modules/
.venv/
venv/
"}

- Additional note

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# System dependencies for building Biopython (C extensions, etc.)
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential python3-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Improve reliability: ensure build tooling is available via pip
RUN python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
RUN python -m pip install --upgrade build

# Set workdir to build in source tree
WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository source code
COPY . /src

# Build both source tarball and wheel from the source
RUN python -m build --sdist
RUN python -m build --wheel

# Install the freshly built distribution(s)
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir dist/*

# Optional: clean up build artifacts to keep the final image small
RUN rm -rf dist Biopython.egg-info __pycache__ tests .tox \
    */__pycache__ */*.pyc

# Default command: quick import test to verify installation
CMD ["bash", "-lc", "python -c 'import Bio, sys; print(\"Biopython version:\", Bio.__version__)'"]
"}

{"path": ".dockerignore", "content": "__pycache__/
*.pyc
.dist/
build/
dist/
*.egg-info/
.eggs/
.eggs-info/
*.tox/
.cache/
*.pytest_cache/
.DS_Store
node_modules/
.venv/
venv/
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# System dependencies for building Biopython (C extensions, etc.)
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential python3-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Improve reliability: ensure build tooling is available via pip
RUN python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
RUN python -m pip install --upgrade build

# Set workdir to build in source tree
WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository source code
COPY . /src

# Build both source tarball and wheel from the source
RUN python -m build --sdist
RUN python -m build --wheel

# Install only the wheel to avoid conflicts with the sdist
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir dist/*.whl

# Remove the source distribution tarball to keep the image clean
RUN rm -f dist/*.tar.gz

# Optional: clean up build artifacts to keep the final image small
# (the wheel is kept because it's installed)
RUN rm -rf Biopython.egg-info __pycache__ tests .tox \
    */__pycache__ */*.pyc

# Default command: quick import test to verify installation
CMD ["bash", "-lc", "python -c 'import Bio, sys; print(\"Biopython version:\", Bio.__version__)'"]
"}

steps.tool_output_raw:
[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=74.1", "wheel"]
build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

[project]
name = "biopython"
dynamic = ["version"]
description = "Freely available tools for computational molecular biology."
readme = "README.rst"
requires-python = ">=3.10"
license = "LicenseRef-Biopython-License-Agreement"
license-files = ["LICENSE.rst"]
authors = [
    {name = "The Biopython Contributors", email = "biopython@biopython.org"}
]
classifiers = [
    "Development Status :: 5 - Production/Stable",
    "Intended Audience :: Developers",
    "Intended Audience :: Science/Research",
    "Operating System :: OS Independent",
    "Programming Language :: Python",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.10",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.11",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.12",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.13",
    "Programming Language :: Python :: 3.14",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering",
    "Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: Bio-Informatics",
    "Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules",
]
dependencies = [
    "numpy",
]

[project.urls]
Homepage = "https://biopython.org/"
Documentation = "https://biopython.org/wiki/Documentation"
Source = "https://github.com/biopython/biopython/"
Tracker = "https://github.com/biopython/biopython/issues"

[tool.setuptools]
include-package-data = true
zip-safe = false
packages = [
    "Bio",
    "Bio.Affy",
    "Bio.Align",
    "Bio.Align.substitution_matrices",
    "Bio.Align.substitution_matrices.data",
    "Bio.AlignIO",
    "Bio.Alphabet",
    "Bio.Blast",
    "Bio.CAPS",
    "Bio.Cluster",
    "Bio.codonalign",
    "Bio.Compass",
    "Bio.Data",
    "Bio.Emboss",
    "Bio.Entrez",
    "Bio.Entrez.DTDs",
    "Bio.Entrez.XSDs",
    "Bio.ExPASy",
    "Bio.GenBank",
    "Bio.Geo",
    "Bio.Graphics",
    "Bio.Graphics.GenomeDiagram",
    "Bio.HMM",
    "Bio.KEGG",
    "Bio.KEGG.Compound",
    "Bio.KEGG.Enzyme",
    "Bio.KEGG.Gene",
    "Bio.KEGG.Map",
    "Bio.KEGG.KGML",
    "Bio.Medline",
    "Bio.motifs",
    "Bio.motifs.jaspar",
    "Bio.Nexus",
    "Bio.NMR",
    "Bio.Pathway",
    "Bio.Pathway.Rep",
    "Bio.PDB",
    "Bio.PDB.mmtf",
    "Bio.phenotype",
    "Bio.PopGen",
    "Bio.PopGen.GenePop",
    "Bio.Restriction",
    "Bio.SCOP",
    "Bio.SearchIO",
    "Bio.SearchIO._model",
    "Bio.SearchIO.BlastIO",
    "Bio.SearchIO.HHsuiteIO",
    "Bio.SearchIO.HmmerIO",
    "Bio.SearchIO.InfernalIO",
    "Bio.SearchIO.ExonerateIO",
    "Bio.SearchIO.InterproscanIO",
    "Bio.SeqIO",
    "Bio.SeqUtils",
    "Bio.Sequencing",
    "Bio.SVDSuperimposer",
    "Bio.SwissProt",
    "Bio.TogoWS",
    "Bio.Phylo",
    "Bio.Phylo.PAML",
    "Bio.UniGene",
    "Bio.UniProt",
    "BioSQL",
]

[tool.setuptools.dynamic]
version = {attr = "Bio.__version__"}

# C extension modules using setuptools 74.1+ native support
[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.Align._aligncore"
sources = ["Bio/Align/_aligncore.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.Align._alignmentcounts"
sources = ["Bio/Align/_alignmentcounts.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.Align._codonaligner"
sources = ["Bio/Align/_codonaligner.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.Align._pairwisealigner"
sources = ["Bio/Align/_pairwisealigner.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.Align.substitution_matrices._arraycore"
sources = ["Bio/Align/substitution_matrices/_arraycore.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.cpairwise2"
sources = ["Bio/cpairwise2module.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.Nexus.cnexus"
sources = ["Bio/Nexus/cnexus.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.motifs._pwm"
sources = ["Bio/motifs/_pwm.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.Cluster._cluster"
sources = ["Bio/Cluster/cluster.c", "Bio/Cluster/clustermodule.c"]
extra-compile-args = ["-DCLUSTER_USE_PYTHON_MEMORY"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.PDB.ccealign"
sources = ["Bio/PDB/ccealignmodule.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.PDB.kdtrees"
sources = ["Bio/PDB/kdtrees.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.PDB._bcif_helper"
sources = ["Bio/PDB/bcifhelpermodule.c"]

[[tool.setuptools.ext-modules]]
name = "Bio.SeqIO._twoBitIO"
sources = ["Bio/SeqIO/_twoBitIO.c"]

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "setuptools>=74.1.0",
]

Actionable summary of the tool output (pyproject.toml for Biopython):

- Build system
  - requires = ["setuptools>=74.1", "wheel"]
  - build-backend = "setuptools.build_meta"

- Project metadata
  - name = "biopython"
  - dynamic = ["version"]
  - description, readme, license, license-files, license info
  - requires-python = ">=3.10"
  - authors: The Biopython Contributors
  - classifiers include Production/Stable, OS Independent, Python 3.10–3.14, Bio-Informatics, etc.
  - dependencies = ["numpy"]

- URLs
  - Homepage, Documentation, Source, Tracker

- Packaging
  - include-package-data = true
  - zip-safe = false
  - packages: long list of Bio.* packages (e.g., Bio, Bio.Affy, Bio.Align, Bio.AlignIO, Bio.Entrez, Bio.PDB, Bio.SeqIO, Bio.SeqUtils, Bio.SwissProt, Bio.TogoWS, Bio.Phylo, Bio.PopGen, BioSQL, etc.)

- Versioning
  - [tool.setuptools.dynamic] version = {attr = "Bio.__version__"}

- C extensions (native modules)
  - Bio.Align._aligncore → Bio/Align/_aligncore.c
  - Bio.Align._alignmentcounts → Bio/Align/_alignmentcounts.c
  - Bio.Align._codonaligner → Bio/Align/_codonaligner.c
  - Bio.Align._pairwisealigner → Bio/Align/_pairwisealigner.c

  - Bio.cpairwise2 → Bio/cpairwise2module.c

  - Bio.Cluster._cluster → Bio/Cluster/cluster.c, Bio/Cluster/clustermodule.c (extra-compile-args = ["-DCLUSTER_USE_PYTHON_MEMORY"])
  - Bio.PDB.ccealign → Bio/PDB/ccealignmodule.c
  - Bio.PDB.kdtrees → Bio/PDB/kdtrees.c
  - Bio.PDB._bcif_helper → Bio/PDB/bcifhelpermodule.c
  - Bio.SeqIO._twoBitIO → Bio/SeqIO/_twoBitIO.c

- More ext-modules exist (under the header “C extension modules using setuptools 74.1+ native support”)

- Dependency groups
  - dev = ["setuptools>=74.1.0"]

Notes:
- Version is dynamically read from Bio.__version__.
- This is a full packaging manifest listing many top-level Bio.* packages and a substantial set of C extensions.

RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir dist/*.whl

RUN rm -f dist/*.whl dist/*.tar.gz || true

# Verification script to ensure Bio is importable and version is shown
RUN bash -lc 'cat > /usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py << "PY"
import Bio
print("Bio version:", getattr(Bio, "__version__", "unknown"))
PY' \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py

# Default command: run the verification script
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm

# System dependencies for building Biopython (C extensions, etc.)
RUN apt-get update \
    && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential python3-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Improve reliability: ensure build tooling is available via pip
RUN python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel
RUN python -m pip install --upgrade build

# Set build context
WORKDIR /src

# Copy source code
COPY . /src

# Build a wheel from source and install it
RUN python -m build --wheel
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir dist/*.whl

# Clean up build artifacts
RUN rm -f dist/*.whl dist/*.tar.gz || true

# Verification script to ensure Bio is importable and version is shown
RUN bash -lc 'cat > /usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py << "PY"\
import Bio\nprint("Bio version:", getattr(Bio, "__version__", "unknown"))\nPY' \
    && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py

# Default command: run the verification script
CMD ["/usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py"]
"}

VerifyBuild status: build_failed
Concerns: Biopython build may require additional runtime/build dependencies (e.g., libbz2, zlib, or specific NumPy headers) depending on the version; ensure the base image includes all prerequisites or validate the build in CI to catch failures., The verification relies on Bio.__version__; if packaging changes or __version__ is not set, the test result could be misleading. Consider also importing a submodule (e.g., Bio.Seq) as a corroborating test., COPY . /src copies the entire repository into the image; without a .dockerignore, build context may be larger than necessary. Adding a .dockerignore to exclude non-source files can speed up builds and reduce image size.

Summary of Docker build error

- Exact error message and exit code
  - Error: ERROR: process "/bin/sh -c bash -lc 'cat > /usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py << "PY" ...' did not complete successfully: exit code: 2
  - Real-time error lines shown:
    - "bash: line 1: warning: here-document at line 1 delimited by end-of-file (wanted `PYimport')"
    - "bash: -c: line 1: `cat > /usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py << "PY"import Bio\nprint("Bio version:", getattr(Bio, "__version__", "unknown"))\nPY'"
    - Final wrap-up: the above RUN failed with exit code 2

- Failing command/step
  - Step: [10/10] RUN bash -lc 'cat > /usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py << "PY"\
    import Bio\nprint("Bio version:", getattr(Bio, "__version__", "unknown"))\nPY'     && chmod +x /usr/local/bin/verify_bio.py
  - The here-document was malformed, causing the shell syntax error and exit code 2.

- Missing packages or files mentioned
  - None reported as missing in the log. The failure is due to a malformed here-document in the final step, not a missing package or file.

- Version mismatch info
  - There is a base-image tag discrepancy observed early in the log:
    - #3 uses FROM docker.io/library/python:3.12-bookworm@sha256:...
    - #4 uses FROM docker.io/library/python:3.12.13-bookworm@sha256:...
  - Both resolve to the same digest (sha256:b06e8541b66390dbe26e635448bfa51b6dd15cdc4d79c5dfd9cacdac8ec5ab91), indicating a tag mismatch with an identical image digest. This could cause confusion about which Python minor version is actually in use.

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Missing `build` module (Step 3)**  
   - **What happened:** Command `RUN python -m build` failed with `/usr/local/bin/python: No module named build`.  
   - **Cause:** `python-build` (PEP 517 build frontend) wasn’t installed.  
   - **Fix:** Before building, install required tools:  
     ```bash
     RUN pip install --no-cache-dir build setuptools wheel
     ```

2. **Local dist artifacts not found (Step 6)**  
   - **What happened:** `python -m pip install --no-cache-dir dist/*` failed (“Cannot open dist/*”).  
   - **Cause:** The previous build didn’t produce `dist/` artifacts or they weren’t copied into the container’s build stage.  
   - **Fix:** Add proper `COPY . .` before `RUN python -m build`, verify `dist/` exists, or directly `pip install .` in editable mode when building inside container.

3. **Smoke test quoting errors (Steps 14, 12)**  
   - **What happened:** `python -c "import Bio; print('ok')"` failed with “Syntax error: Unterminated quoted string”. Earlier, a here‑doc script used literal “\n”.  
   - **Cause:** Improper quoting inside RUN or echo commands.  
   - **Fix:** Use single‑quoted shell strings; for embedded Python scripts use `<<'EOF' … EOF` to preserve newlines literally.

4. **Python header mismatch & missing build deps (Steps 18, 20)**  
   - **What happened:** Build failed when compiling C extensions due to missing or mismatched Python headers.  
   - **Fix:** Since the base image already includes correct headers for that interpreter, **don’t install `python3-dev`**; instead install compilation essentials only:  
     ```bash
     apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential libz-dev libbz2-dev libssl-dev libffi-dev
     ```

5. **NumPy required during build (Step 23)**  
   - **What happened:** Build failed because Biopython’s C extensions depend on NumPy headers.  
   - **Fix:** Pre‑install NumPy before building Biopython:  
     ```bash
     RUN pip install --no-cache-dir numpy
     ```

6. **Incorrect module reference during smoke test (Steps 8, 16)**  
   - **What happened:** Smoke test expected `Bio.__version__`, which isn’t guaranteed in all Biopython versions.  
   - **Fix:** Validate installation more robustly, e.g.:  
     ```bash
     RUN python - <<'EOF'\nimport Bio.Seq; print("Biopython OK")\nEOF
     ```

7. **Context bloat concern (Step 10)**  
   - **What happened:** No `.dockerignore`; build context was large.  
   - **Fix:** Keep `.dockerignore` excluding cache, venv, build, and test artifacts.

8. **General optimization**  
   - Use multi‑stage build: stage 1 builds wheel (`python -m build`), stage 2 installs wheel into minimal runtime image.  
   - Always chain `apt-get clean` and `rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*` to shrink image.  
   - Verify success with `python -m Bio` or a small import test, not `Bio.__version__`.

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS builder

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
WORKDIR /src

# Install essential build tools and headers for compiling C extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libbz2-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libffi-dev \
      ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Python packaging/build tools
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip setuptools wheel build

# Copy source code and build from source
COPY . .

# Build sdist and wheel from source
RUN python -m build --sdist && python -m build --wheel

# Runtime stage
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
WORKDIR /app

# Copy built artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /src/dist /dist

# Install runtime dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install NumPy for runtime compatibility with Biopython
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir numpy

# Install the built distribution wheels
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir /dist/*.whl

# Smoke test to verify installation during image build
RUN python -c "import Bio, sys; print('Biopython installed, version:', Bio.__version__)"

# Default command: verify import at container start
CMD ["python", "-c", "import Bio, sys; print('Biopython version:', Bio.__version__)" ]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS builder

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
WORKDIR /src

# Install essential build tools and headers for compiling C extensions
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
      build-essential \
      libbz2-dev \
      zlib1g-dev \
      liblzma-dev \
      libssl-dev \
      libffi-dev \
      ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Python packaging/build tools
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip setuptools wheel build

# Copy source code and build from source
COPY . .

# Build sdist and wheel from source
RUN python -m build --sdist && python -m build --wheel

# Runtime stage
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1
WORKDIR /app

# Copy built artifacts from builder
COPY --from=builder /src/dist /dist

# Install runtime dependencies
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# The wheel's dependencies (like numpy) will be installed automatically
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir /dist/*.whl

# Lightweight verify script to confirm Biopython is importable
RUN printf "import Bio\nprint('Biopython version', Bio.__version__)\n" > /verify_bio.py
CMD ["python", "/verify_bio.py"]
"}

iterations.injected_lessons:
1. **Build Context Problems**  
   - **What happened:** Early smoke failures mentioned that `COPY . .` might have copied the entire repo, bloating context size.  
   - **Cause:** `.dockerignore` may not exclude enough; unnecessary files (e.g., data, virtualenvs, `.git`) increase image size and slow builds.  
   - **Fix:** Audit `.dockerignore` to keep only source code, `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, and necessary scripts. Exclude `tests/`, `docs/`, and cached artifacts.

2. **Biopython Import Test Syntax Error**  
   - **What happened:** Every smoke test failed with `/bin/sh: 1: Syntax error: Unterminated quoted string` or `word unexpected (expecting ")")`.  
   - **Cause:** Incorrect quoting in the test command—lines such as  
     `python -c 'import Bio; print(Bio.__version__)`  
     lack a closing quote. Some steps used mixed quoting (`"`) or missing end parentheses.  
   - **Fix:** Use valid shell syntax:  
     ```sh
     python -c "import Bio; print(Bio.__version__)"
     ```  
     or  
     ```sh
     python -c 'import Bio; print(Bio.__version__)'
     ```  
     Ensure all parentheses and quotes close properly.

3. **Biopython Availability**  
   - **What happened:** Multiple rebuilds aimed to get Biopython working but tests failed before confirming import success.  
   - **Cause:** Potential mismatch between build‑time installs and runtime environment (installation may happen in builder but not copied, or dependency incomplete).  
   - **Fix:**  
     - Install Biopython explicitly in the runtime stage: `RUN pip install biopython==<desired_version>`.  
     - Verify `Bio` imports after installation.  
     - If using wheels built in the builder stage, ensure `COPY --from=builder` moves them correctly.

4. **Unnecessary NumPy Installation**  
   - **What happened:** Notes indicated NumPy was installed unconditionally even if Biopython’s wheel already bundles it.  
   - **Fix:** Remove redundant `pip install numpy` unless it’s explicitly required by your code. Let Biopython manage it via its dependencies.

5. **Runtime Verification Steps**  
   - **What happened:** All smoke tests failed before actually validating library versions.  
   - **Fix:** After installing dependencies, run a simple command to confirm environment readiness in the Dockerfile itself, e.g.:  
     ```dockerfile
     RUN python -c "import Bio; print(Bio.__version__)"
     ```  
     This catches missing packages or syntax issues early.

6. **Final Check**  
   - Ensure the Dockerfile’s final stage runs under a lightweight Python base (e.g., `python:3.12-slim-bookworm`) after building wheels, and that environment variables are preserved (`PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE`, `PYTHONUNBUFFERED`).

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS builder

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Install build tools required for compiling C extensions (e.g., Biopython)
RUN set -eux; \
    apt-get update; \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential python3-dev; \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository source
COPY . .

# Ensure build tooling is available
RUN python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel build

# Build both source tarball and wheel from the repository
RUN python -m build --sdist
RUN python -m build --wheel

# Runtime stage
FROM python:3.12-slim-bookworm AS runtime

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Lightweight runtime: include only necessary system deps
RUN set -eux; \
    apt-get update; \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates; \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Copy the repository into the runtime and install in editable mode
COPY --from=builder /src /app

# Install the repository in editable mode so imports resolve cleanly in this environment
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir -e /app

# Verification: a quick import to ensure the package is importable
RUN python -c "import Bio; print('Bio version:', Bio.__version__)"

# Default command: demonstrate a simple import of the library
CMD ["python", "-c", "import Bio; print(Bio.__version__)"]
"}

steps.tool_output_raw:
VerifyBuild status: build_failed
Concerns: Potential reliance on Bio.__version__ being defined; if packaging changes this attribute may not exist., The build uses editable install (-e /app); if package metadata changes, installation may fail.
Build error:
Actionable summary of the Docker build failure

- Exact error message and exit code
  - exit code: 1
  - error: command 'gcc' failed: No such file or directory
  - Context snippet: Building editable for biopython (pyproject.toml) did not run successfully.

- Failing command/step
  - Failing step: [runtime 5/6] RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir -e /app
  - Core failure during editable install of the app (biopython) inside the runtime stage.

- Missing packages or files mentioned
  - GCC/build tools missing in the runtime image (No such file or directory: gcc).
  - Implication: required native extensions for Biopython could not be compiled.
  - Builder stage earlier had build tools installed (build-essential python3-dev), but these are not present in the final runtime image.

- Version mismatch information
  - Runtime base: python:3.12-slim-bookworm
  - Build artifacts reference: Biopython 1.87.dev0 (editable install from /app)
  - Python ABI in logs: cp312 (Python 3.12)
  - NumPy wheel retrieved: numpy-2.4.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_27_x86_64.whl
  - Observed multi-stage setup includes separate builder and runtime images (builder stages installed build tools; runtime stage did not).

- What happened, succinctly
  - The pipeline attempted to install the app in editable mode, which triggers building Biopython from source and compiling C extensions with gcc. The runtime image lacks gcc, so the build fails with “gcc: No such file or directory,” causing the entire pip install to fail (exit code 1).

- Quick fixes you can apply
  - Install build tools in the runtime image before the editable install:
    - apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential gcc
  - Optionally also install python3-dev and common build deps (e.g., libffi-dev, zlib1g-dev, libxml2-dev) if Biopython requires them.
  - Alternatively avoid editable install: install a prebuilt Biopython wheel from PyPI or adjust to a non-editable install if appropriate.
  - Ensure consistency between builder and runtime environments, so tools from the builder stage are available in the final image (or consolidate into a single stage that includes build tools).

verify_build_details.build_error:
Actionable summary of the Docker build failure

- Exact error message and exit code
  - exit code: 1
  - error: command 'gcc' failed: No such file or directory
  - Context snippet: Building editable for biopython (pyproject.toml) did not run successfully.

- Failing command/step
  - Failing step: [runtime 5/6] RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir -e /app
  - Core failure during editable install of the app (biopython) inside the runtime stage.

- Missing packages or files mentioned
  - GCC/build tools missing in the runtime image (No such file or directory: gcc).
  - Implication: required native extensions for Biopython could not be compiled.
  - Builder stage earlier had build tools installed (build-essential python3-dev), but these are not present in the final runtime image.

- Version mismatch information
  - Runtime base: python:3.12-slim-bookworm
  - Build artifacts reference: Biopython 1.87.dev0 (editable install from /app)
  - Python ABI in logs: cp312 (Python 3.12)
  - NumPy wheel retrieved: numpy-2.4.3-cp312-cp312-manylinux_2_27_x86_64.whl
  - Observed multi-stage setup includes separate builder and runtime images (builder stages installed build tools; runtime stage did not).

- What happened, succinctly
  - The pipeline attempted to install the app in editable mode, which triggers building Biopython from source and compiling C extensions with gcc. The runtime image lacks gcc, so the build fails with “gcc: No such file or directory,” causing the entire pip install to fail (exit code 1).

- Quick fixes you can apply
  - Install build tools in the runtime image before the editable install:
    - apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential gcc
  - Optionally also install python3-dev and common build deps (e.g., libffi-dev, zlib1g-dev, libxml2-dev) if Biopython requires them.
  - Alternatively avoid editable install: install a prebuilt Biopython wheel from PyPI or adjust to a non-editable install if appropriate.
  - Ensure consistency between builder and runtime environments, so tools from the builder stage are available in the final image (or consolidate into a single stage that includes build tools).

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "# syntax=docker/dockerfile:1
FROM python:3.12.13-bookworm AS builder

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Install build tools required for compiling C extensions (e.g., Biopython)
RUN set -eux; \
    apt-get update; \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential python3-dev; \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository source
COPY . .

# Ensure build tooling is available
RUN python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools wheel build

# Build both source tarball and wheel from the repository
RUN python -m build --sdist
RUN python -m build --wheel

# Runtime stage
FROM python:3.12-slim-bookworm AS runtime

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1
ENV PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1

# Lightweight runtime: include only necessary system deps
RUN set -eux; \
    apt-get update; \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates; \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

# Copy built artifacts from the builder
COPY --from=builder /src/dist /dist

# Install the built distribution(s) (no editable install)
RUN python -m pip install --no-cache-dir /dist/*.whl

# Verification: a quick import to ensure the package is importable
RUN python -c "import Bio; print('Bio version:', Bio.__version__)"

# Default command: demonstration of importing Bio
CMD ["python", "-c", "import Bio; print(Bio.__version__)"]
"}
